name: Conviva Streaming Analytics API
what_it_does: Conviva Streaming Analytics REST API for video quality of experience (QoE), audience measurement, and OTT streaming performance analytics. Enables AI agents to manage real-time video playback quality monitoring for streaming QoE automation, handle audience engagement and concurrent viewer analytics for content performance tracking, access video start failure and buffering ratio analysis for streaming reliability monitoring, retrieve geographic and device-level QoE breakdown for CDN performance optimization, manage content and ad insertion performance analytics for monetization optimization, handle live streaming quality monitoring for broadcast event analytics, access social and marketing data integration for cross-channel audience analytics, retrieve churn prediction and retention analytics for subscriber management, manage CDN selection and ABR optimization for streaming delivery automation, and integrate Conviva with video players, CDNs, and advertising platforms.
use_cases: Monitoring video QoE from streaming operations agents
Tracking concurrent viewers from content analytics agents
Optimizing CDN performance from delivery agents
Detecting buffering issues from reliability agents
not_for: Audio-only platforms without video quality measurement
On-premises video delivery without cloud analytics
best_when: A streaming media company, broadcaster, or OTT platform wants AI agents to automate video QoE monitoring, audience measurement, CDN performance optimization, and streaming reliability alerting.
avoid_when: REAL-TIME DATA PROCESSING LATENCY: Conviva real-time streaming analytics has inherent latency in data processing; automated QoE alerts and CDN switching decisions must account for data ingestion lag; near-real-time automation is possible but truly real-time (sub-second) CDN switching requires edge-level logic not API-level. Privacy and audience measurement compliance — streaming audience data includes viewing history that triggers VPPA (Video Privacy Protection Act) requirements; automated audience analytics must comply with VPPA opt-out requirements and state privacy laws (CCPA, CPRA). Ad insertion measurement — automated ad performance analytics must distinguish fill rate issues (ad server) from delivery issues (CDN/player); automated optimization without root cause isolation creates incorrect remediation.

auth.notes: Conviva uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Documentation at developer.conviva.com. SDKs for video players. Foster City, California HQ. Founded 2006. Private (Norwest Venture Partners, Bessemer, Intel Capital). 300+ streaming media customers including major broadcasters and OTT platforms. Measures 500M+ viewers. Competes with Mux, Akamai mPulse, and Nielsen for streaming analytics.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-viewer-hour or enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-viewer-hour pricing; enterprise subscription for large platforms
pricing.notes: Foster City, California. Founded 2006. Norwest/Bessemer/Intel-backed. 300+ streaming customers. 500M+ viewers measured. Streaming QoE analytics. Competes with Mux and Akamai for video analytics.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; real-time data has inherent processing lag

known_agent_gotchas: VPPA VIEWING HISTORY COMPLIANCE: Streaming audience analytics includes individual viewing history triggering VPPA protections; automated audience data processing must comply with VPPA opt-out requirements and state privacy laws (CCPA, CPRA) for California subscribers

Real-time data processing lag — Conviva analytics data has inherent ingestion and processing latency (seconds to minutes); automated CDN switching or QoE alerting must account for data lag; true sub-second automation requires edge-level logic

Ad insertion root cause separation — automated ad performance analysis must distinguish ad server fill failures from CDN/player delivery issues; automated optimization without proper root cause isolation creates incorrect remediation actions

Player SDK instrumentation requirement — Conviva analytics requires SDK integration in video players; API analytics are dependent on correct SDK instrumentation; gaps in SDK coverage create blind spots in QoE reporting

CDN complexity in analytics — multi-CDN delivery creates attribution complexity in QoE metrics; automated CDN performance comparison must account for different traffic mixes and geographic distributions across CDNs

Live vs VOD streaming differences — live streaming QoE metrics and thresholds differ significantly from VOD; automated alerting thresholds must be tuned separately for live event versus on-demand content
agent_readiness.security_notes: Streaming analytics. SOC2, ISO27001. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Streaming audience and viewing history data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Streaming analytics. SOC2, ISO27001. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Viewer and streaming data.
